content.principles: 1. Slow Start: Exponential growth of cwnd (congestion window). 2. Congestion Avoidance: Linear growth once cwnd reaches ssthresh. 3. Fast Retransmit: Responding to 3 duplicate ACKs. 4. Fast Recovery: Adjusting ssthresh and cwnd instead of dropping to slow start. BBR (Bottleneck Bandwidth and RTT) is a modern alternative focusing on throughput.
pitfalls: Packet loss being interpreted as congestion in high-loss wireless networks.
Bufferbloat: Large buffers causing high latency without dropping packets.
TCP global synchronization when multiple flows drop cwnd simultaneously.
